data.title: U.S. Spot Crypto ETFs Saw Strong Inflows on Wednesday, JPMorgan Says
data.description:
• U.S. spot crypto ETFs saw strong inflows on Wednesday, according to JPMorgan.
  • The bank estimated spot ether ETF net sales of $84 million, and bitcoin sales of $431 million.

data.title: Peter Schiff Calls Bitcoin a 'Giant Cult,' Doubles Down on Gold
data.description:
• Peter Schiff said the Bitcoin community behaves like a cult driven more by hype than by substance.
  • He argued that central banks still buying gold proves the yellow metal remains the preferred reserve asset.
  • Schiff joked that by warning people against Bitcoin, he may have convinced more to buy it.

data.title: Bitmain's New Antminer Launch Signals Profound Shift for Bitcoin Miners: TheMinerMag
data.description:
• Bitmain’s Antminer S23 Hydro features 9.7 J/TH efficiency, shipping in Q1 2026.
  • Miners may focus on replacing old rigs instead of expanding fleets amid profitability squeeze.
  • Hashprice remains under pressure, challenging conventional hardware upgrade cycles.

data.title: Bitcoin Treasury Firm Twenty One Capital Brings Total Fundraise to $685M
data.description:
• Bitcoin treasury firm Twenty One has raised an additional $100 million through convertible senior secured notes, increasing its total capital raised to $685 million.
  • The new financing comes from existing investors and sponsors who exercised their option to buy more notes, originally granted during the April fundraising round, according to an SEC filing.
  • Twenty One Capital is being launched by Cantor's Brandon Lutnick via a SPAC structure, with notable owners including iFinex and Tether, and will be led by Strike CEO Jack Mallers.

data.title: Tether, Tron Dominate Fast-Growing Stablecoin Payments Arena, Survey Shows
data.description:
• The “Stablecoin Payments from the Ground Up” report found USDT accounted for 90% of payment transaction volume.
  • Tron was the preferred settlement network, hosting around 60% of volume.
  • Stablecoin payment volume taken in February of this year added up to an annualized $72.3 billion.

data.title: UNI Surges 10% This Week as Bitcoin Consolidates
data.description:
• UNI has risen by 10% this week, rising to as high as $7.63 on Thursday before moving back to test the $7.00 level of support.
  • It comes as bitcoin continues to consolidate at $108,000 after it formed a new record high last week.
  • CoinMarketCap's altcoin season index is currently at 25 after being as low as 12 last month.

data.title: CANTO Rises From the Ashes, Spiking 250% Before Crashing Back Down
data.description:
• CANTO experienced a period of volatility on Thursday, rising by 250% before crashing back down over the subsequent six hours.
  • The blockchain had more than $200 million in total value locked (TVL) last year before a network outage caused an exodus of capital.
  • Thursday's move can be attributed to an impulse in buy pressure coupled with a lack of liquidity.

data.title: Telegram Raises $1.7B Via Convertible Bonds: Bloomberg
data.description:
• Telegram raised $1.7 billion by issuing convertible bonds.
  • $955 million will be used for bond buyback and $745 million for operations and growth.
  • Investors can convert bonds to equity if Telegram goes public before maturity.

data.title: Solana's 18 Month Long Bull Run Against Ether is Over; XRP Ends Mini-Uptrend
data.description:
• The SOL/ETH ratio signals end of a prolonged uptrend and ether outperformance ahead.
  • XRP's recovery from April lows looks to be over.

data.title: BlackRock Mulling 10% Stake in Circle’s IPO, Joining ARK as Potential Buyer: Bloomberg
data.description:
• BlackRock is considering buying 10% of the shares in Circle's IPO, according to Bloomberg.
  • Circle filed its IPO paperwork on Tuesday, offering 24 million shares split between the company and its stakeholders.
  • ARK Invest has indicated interest in purchasing $150 million worth of shares in the offering.
